title: CLI Authority Proof Routing
goal: Implement #447 so proof selection surfaces CLI authority before generated or executable CLI edits.
non_goals: Do not build the broader CLI code generation roadmap in this slice.
Do not change CLI implementation ownership semantics unless the proof lane exposes a real inconsistency.

intent.outcome: Agents touching CLI files can tell whether the file is source, generator, projection, or hand-owned executable code before implementation starts.
intent.constraints: Keep this bounded to proof/startup authority routing and tests.
intent.latitude: Choose the smallest contract shape that lets proof selection answer direct-edit authority.
intent.escalation: Escalate if the change requires replacing the current CLI architecture.

execution.next_step: Inspect existing proof lane selection and CLI command/contract metadata.
execution.proof: focused proof selection tests plus workspace CLI validation

invariants: CLI authority must be visible before edits, not reconstructed after user challenge.
Generated or projected CLI slices should name source contract and regeneration path before being treated as safe.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: #442: make the plan -> implement -> evaluate continuation loop first-class for long lanes.

required_continuation.required follow-on detail: #448 remains open after this slice, and #442 remains open until the loop itself is first-class.

parent_lane.title: Add an autopilot intent loop for plan, implement, evaluate, and continue

iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: continue the current milestone until the completion criteria are met

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Surface CLI authority before generated or executable CLI edits.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Do not let generated/projection CLI slices look equivalent to hand-owned executable code.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: The exact proof field names and authority categories.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: The implementation requires replacing the CLI architecture or introducing new package-generation machinery.

context_budget.live working set: state, this execplan, #447 issue, proof selector metadata, CLI command contracts, and workspace CLI tests
context_budget.tiny resumability note: Next active issue is #447 CLI authority proof routing.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Before changing CLI ownership semantics or adding generated CLI machinery.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Implementation should start from compact authority surfaces and avoid manual after-the-fact validation.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Expose authority in existing proof routing rather than adding a new first-contact surface.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Did this slice make the package cheaper and safer to use before direct CLI edits?

active_milestone.scope: Keep this execution thread bounded to the promoted TODO item.

immediate_next_action: Inspect CLI command contracts and any generated adapter metadata.
Add tests for generated/projection and hand-owned CLI path classification.
invariants: Preserve the planning contract and keep the work bounded to this plan.
validation_commands: uv run ruff check src tests
uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json
completion_criteria: agentic-workspace proof --changed <cli paths> selects a CLI authority lane when appropriate.
The selected proof states whether the changed file is source, generator, projection, or hand-owned code.
Generated/projection CLI slices fail early unless their source contract and regeneration path are named.
Tests cover at least one generated CLI path and one hand-owned CLI path.

closure_check.evidence carried forward: #445 external intent refresh adapter and #446 payload drift classification landed.
closure_check.reopen trigger: proof selection still does not expose CLI authority before direct CLI edits
